title: silene otites subsp. pseudotites
intro: Silene otites subsp. pseudotites, also known as the false ear-leaved catchfly, is a perennial plant species within the carnation family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating sore throats, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
